Finding: No CI gate preventing silent removal of JWT security via operation-level security:[]
Mitigation: Add Spectral lint rule oas-security-defined to CI; add validate-jwt policy in Azure API Management as defence-in-depth

Finding: BOLA: any authenticated user can update any loan status or read any book's borrower history without ownership check
Mitigation: Resolve JWT sub claim in middleware; assert caller is loan.borrower.id or book.owner.id before allowing status update; restrict history to book owner only; return 403 on mismatch

Finding: Free-text search parameter q has no length or character constraint and is susceptible to SQL injection if not parameterised
Mitigation: Enforce parameterised queries / ORM; add maxLength:200 to q schema in OpenAPI spec; run SAST in CI

Finding: sl-violations header echoes request field values in error responses; connection strings and storage keys have no declared secret management
Mitigation: Store secrets in Azure Key Vault with managed identity; suppress sl-violations header in production; enable secret scanning in CI

Finding: API served over HTTP only – TLS not enforced; ZAP confirmed HTTPS connection refused on mock (CWE-311)
Mitigation: Configure Azure Front Door HTTP→HTTPS redirect; set minimumTlsVersion:TLS12; add HSTS header; update OpenAPI servers URL to https://

Finding: No rate-limiting on write or loan-request endpoints; no Retry-After or X-RateLimit headers observed in ZAP scan
Mitigation: Apply rate-limit-by-key policy in Azure API Management scoped to JWT sub: 10 req/min on loan requests; 5 req/min on book creation; return 429 with Retry-After

Finding: Loan history exposes borrower displayName and UUID to any authenticated caller; Prism sl-violations header may surface PII in logs
Mitigation: Restrict loan history to book owner; redact borrower.id in non-owner responses; configure Application Insights telemetry processor to hash PII; apply RBAC on log access

Finding: ZAP Alert: HTTP Only Site (Medium / CWE-311) – server did not respond on HTTPS during baseline scan
Mitigation: Enforce TLS at Azure Front Door; configure Prism mock with TLS cert for local testing parity

Finding: ZAP Alert: Cross-Domain Misconfiguration (Medium / CWE-264) – CORS headers use wildcard origin combined with Allow-Credentials:true on POST /books
Mitigation: Set Access-Control-Allow-Origin to exact production front-end origin in Azure API Management CORS policy; remove wildcard
